chief_complaint: A 70-year-old man presented with acute dyspnea and chest pain 7 months after initiation of olaparib therapy for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.
history: The patient had a history of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) with a BRCA2 mutation. He was started on olaparib, a poly (ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibitor. He developed acute dyspnea and chest pain 7 months after olaparib initiation.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: Diagnostic evaluations included contrast-enhanced computed tomography and serum D-dimer level measurement. Imaging confirmed multiple pulmonary emboli; laboratory testing revealed markedly elevated D-dimer levels.
